parameters-en.RoomSize: Selects the size of the hall (8, 10, or 12 meters).
parameters-en.Predelay: Controls the amount of delay heard before the signal enters the hall. Can sometimes result in more definition between the dry and effected signals.
parameters-en.Damping: Determines the frequency above which the reverb will be absorbed. For example, if your hall is full of people wearing fake ocelot jumpsuits, more high frequencies would be absorbed than if the room were empty.
parameters-en.Diffusion: Controls the amount of smearing between discrete echoes, sometimes resulting in a softer effected signal.
parameters-en.Shape: Controls the blend of the Early and Late reflections. Turning the knob clockwise adds more Late reflections; turning the knob counterclockwise adds more Early reflections.
parameters-en.Mix: Controls the blend between the reverb and the dry signal. At 0%, no reverb is heard; at 100%, no dry signal is heard.
parameters-en.Low Cut: Applies a low cut (or high pass) filter to the reverb, letting you remove the effected signal below a certain frequency.
parameters-en.High Cut: Applies a high cut (or low pass) filter to the reverb, letting you remove the effected signal above a certain frequency
parameters-en.Level: Sets the overall level of the block.
